title: What to know about Ruscus aculeatus before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Ruscus aculeatus are alleviating varicose veins due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. Ruscus aculeatus is also used to treat edema, as it contains flavonoids that exhibit vasoconstrictive properties, reducing fluid accumulation in the body. Its use in treating diarrhea is attributed to its tannin content, which has astringent properties, reducing intestinal secretions and inflammation. The plant's anti-inflammatory flavonoids and saponins also make it effective in treating arthritis, while its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties make it useful in alleviating hemorrhoids. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus's flavonoids have antipyretic properties, making it a treatment for fever, and its anti-inflammatory properties also relieve rheumatism, digestive issues, vata imbalance, and gout.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Ruscus aculeatus are multifaceted, primarily exhibiting antimicrobial activity due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which inhibit the growth of various pathogens. Ruscus aculeatus also possesses antioxidant properties, attributed to the high content of saponins and other polyphenols, which neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, thus exerting a protective effect against chronic diseases. Additionally, the plant shows anti-inflammatory properties, which are mediated by sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis. Ruscus aculeatus has been found to exhibit analgesic, antispasmodic, and vasoconstrictor properties, likely due to the presence of volatile oils and alkaloids, which contribute to its pain-relieving and muscle-relaxing effects. Furthermore, the plant displays cardioactive and diuretic properties, likely attributed to the presence of glycosides and other bioactive compounds, which modulate cardiovascular function and promote urine production, respectively, and its sedative properties are thought to be mediated by the presence of alkaloids, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.
identification: Ruscus aculeatus is a perennial, evergreen shrub with a woody base and dark green, glossy, leathery leaves that are 5-10 cm long, lanceolate to ovate in shape, and have a distinctive aculeate (prickly) margin. The plant typically grows 1-2 meters tall, with a spread of up to 1 meter, and has a slow to moderate growth rate. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula, North Africa, and parts of southern Europe, where it inhabits scrublands, rocky outcrops, and woodland edges in well-drained soils. Ruscus aculeatus is a member of the Asparagaceae family and is often found in association with other shrubs and trees, such as juniper and oak. Its geographical range has been expanded through cultivation, and it is now grown as an ornamental plant in many parts of the world.
cultivation: Ruscus aculeatus is a shade-tolerant, evergreen perennial that thrives in a range of growth habits from upright shrubs to low-maintenance groundcovers, making it suitable for various landscaping applications. It grows best in well-drained soils with a slightly acidic pH (5.5-6.5) and consistent moisture levels, but can tolerate some drought and a wide range of temperatures (2-25°C). To promote healthy growth, it's essential to provide Ruscus with partial shade, especially in warmer climates, and avoid over-fertilizing, as this can lead to weak and leggy stems. Regular pruning is also crucial to maintain its desired shape and promote new growth, typically done in late winter or early spring when the plant is dormant. By following these growth requirements and tips, Ruscus aculeatus can be a low-maintenance and adaptable addition to a variety of gardens and landscapes.
history: Ruscus aculeatus is a plant whose historical uses as a medicine date back to ancient times. The ancient Greeks and Romans used it to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gout. In the 16th and 17th centuries, the plant was used to treat indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues. Ruscus aculeatus was also used as a diuretic and to treat conditions such as dropsy and edema. The plant's medicinal properties were also documented by the 18th-century Swedish botanist Carolus Linnaeus.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: A group of glycosides found in Ruscus aculeatus, characterized by their foaming and detergent properties, and the ability to form complexes with proteins and other biomolecules.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids from Ruscus aculeatus have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a class of phenolic compounds found in Ruscus aculeatus with anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids, found in Ruscus aculeatus, are water-soluble compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Ruscus aculeatus glycosides are reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids from Ruscus aculeatus possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Ruscus aculeatus alkaloids have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties.

constituents.name: Steroids
constituents.description: Steroids from Ruscus aculeatus are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory compounds found in Ruscus aculeatus.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Sterols from Ruscus aculeatus are known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and may also exhibit vasodilatory and hepatoprotective effects.

constituents.name: Triterpenes
constituents.description: Triterpenes are constituents of Ruscus aculeatus that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Terpenoids found in Ruscus aculeatus have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides from Ruscus aculeatus are flavonoid compounds with antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Furanocoumarins
constituents.description: A class of compounds found in Ruscus aculeatus, characterized by their furan ring structure and coumarin moiety, possessing phototoxic and antiproliferative properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are a class of compounds found in Ruscus aculeatus that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, commonly found in Ruscus aculeatus.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid known for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties.

constituents.name: Fatty acids
constituents.description: Fatty acids, present in Ruscus aculeatus, exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Sesquiterpenes
constituents.description: Sesquiterpenes are present in Ruscus aculeatus with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Amino acids
constituents.description: Amino acids present in Ruscus aculeatus are known to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-modulating properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Allergic reactions to Ruscus aculeatus sap can be exacerbated by skin contact.
Rhus toxicodendron (Poison Ivy) and Rhus radicans (Poison Sumac) cross-reactivity can occur with Ruscus aculeatus, exacerbating symptoms in sensitive individuals.
Causing skin inflammation in people sensitive to its sap or other components.
Ruscus aculeatus causes urticaria in some individuals
Allergic reactions worsen symptoms of eczema and asthma.
Ruscus aculeatus toxicity can cause increased neuromuscular excitability
Contact with sap of Ruscus aculeatus can cause skin irritation in some individuals.
Increases alertness and arousal through neurostimulatory effects
Ruscus aculeatus causes increased sensitivity to allergens or irritants, potentially leading to allergic reactions

intro_description: Ruscus aculeatus, commonly known as butcher's broom, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions. It has been traditionally used to help with circulatory issues, including varicose veins and hemorrhoids, as well as swelling and edema. Main health benefits of Ruscus aculeatus include its anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which help to improve circulation and reduce blood pressure. The herb's therapeutic properties include its ability to improve venous tone, reduce capillary permeability, and enhance fibrinolysis. The bioactive compounds responsible for these effects include ruscogenin, isoruscogenin, and ruscic acid, which are commonly used to make a herbal preparation known as butcher's broom extract.

uses_list.description: The leaves and roots of this plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain in joints affected by arthritis, making it a useful natural remedy for the condition.
The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat fever due to their ability to help lower body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with it.
This plant's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help to relieve the pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with rheumatism, making it a useful natural remedy for the condition.
The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their bronchodilatory properties, which help to ease breathing and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.
The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat diarrhea due to their astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.
The plant's anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties help to relieve the itching, redness, and inflammation associated with eczema, making it a useful natural remedy for the condition.

The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat varicose veins due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.
The plant's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease pain in joints affected by gout, making it a useful natural remedy for the condition.

The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.

The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat diabetes due to their hypoglycemic properties, which help to lower blood sugar levels and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including eczema, a skin condition characterized by inflammation and itching, as well as rheumatism, which involves joint pain and inflammation. Ruscus aculeatus is also used to alleviate the symptoms of arthritis, a degenerative joint disease that causes pain and stiffness. Furthermore, it is utilized in the treatment of varicose veins, a condition in which veins become swollen and twisted, and skin inflammation, which can be caused by various factors. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus is used to manage gout, a type of arthritis that causes sudden and severe joint pain, as well as diarrhea, a condition characterized by frequent bowel movements. Its therapeutic properties also extend to the treatment of fever, asthma, hypertension, hemorrhoids, psoriasis, osteoarthritis, skin irritation, diabetes, varicose ulcers, muscle pain, digestive issues, vomiting, and cancer.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat diarrhea and fever by eliminating pathogens and cooling the body. Ruscus aculeatus is also used to relieve rheumatism and inflammation by reducing joint pain and swelling, as well as asthma by opening airways and reducing mucus production. Additionally, it is used to treat nausea and vomiting by regulating the digestive system, and arthritis by reducing pain and stiffness. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat eczema by regulating skin function, and hypertension by reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. It is also used to treat dysentery by eliminating pathogens, cough by reducing mucus production, dyspepsia by regulating digestion, diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels, epilepsy by reducing seizures, gout by reducing uric acid levels, malaria by eliminating parasites, migraine by reducing pain and inflammation, osteoporosis by regulating bone function, and anemia by improving iron absorption, as well as to relieve eczema.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, which it helps alleviate through its anti-inflammatory properties, as well as rheumatism, by reducing joint pain and inflammation. It's also effective in managing asthma, particularly in its acute symptoms, and skin inflammation, such as eczema, by providing relief from itching and redness. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus is used to lower fever, reduce skin irritation, and treat diarrhea, varicose veins, and insect bites, while its antiseptic properties aid in wound healing. Additionally, it's used to manage conditions like psoriasis, gout, and hemorrhoids, as well as to reduce hypertension and gastroenteritis, and to alleviate symptoms of anemia, headache, and burns. Its therapeutic applications also extend to treating eczema, gout, and gastroenteritis.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat a variety of health conditions. The plant is traditionally used to treat asthma by reducing inflammation and relaxing airway spasms. It's also employed to alleviate fever through its diaphoretic properties and reduce the severity of inflammatory arthritis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat eczema, diarrhea, skin infections, rheumatism, gout, diabetes, hypertension, psoriasis, hemorrhoids, varicose veins, chronic cough, kidney stones, and vomiting due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. Its effectiveness in treating headache, indigestion, gastritis, and inflammation, particularly in the form of its extracts, has also been well-documented.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat arthritis, a condition characterized by joint inflammation and pain. The plant is also employed to alleviate varicose veins, a circulatory disorder that affects the veins in the legs. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus is used to manage eczema, an inflammatory skin condition. It is also a common treatment for inflammation, a response to injury or infection, and is used to treat gout, a form of arthritis caused by excess uric acid in the blood. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus is used to treat rheumatism, diarrhea, asthma, insect bites, bruises, skin irritation, psoriasis, hemorrhoids, fever, cuts, hypertension, sprains, wounds, burns, and diabetes, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

benefits_list.description: This benefit involves the ability to suppress the division and proliferation of cells, which can be beneficial in preventing the growth of cancer cells and other unwanted cellular processes. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can interact with cellular mechanisms to inhibit cell growth, thereby exerting a potential therapeutic effect.

Oxidative stress occurs when there is an imbalance between the production of free radicals and the body's ability to neutralize them, leading to cellular damage. This plant contains antioxidants that can scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health.

Apoptosis is a process of programmed cell death that helps to eliminate damaged or unwanted cells from the body. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can induce apoptosis in certain cell types, thereby promoting the elimination of damaged cells and preventing the development of diseases.

Tumor formation involves the uncontrolled growth and division of cells, leading to the development of cancer. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can inhibit tumor formation by suppressing cell growth, inducing apoptosis, and preventing the formation of new blood vessels that supply tumors with nutrients.

Antimicrobial activity involves the ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and viruses. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can display antimicrobial activity by interacting with microbial cell membranes, interfering with their metabolic processes, and ultimately killing or inhibiting their growth.

Inflammation is a response to tissue damage or infection, characterized by the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and the activation of immune cells. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can demonstrate anti-inflammatory effects by suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines, inhibiting the activation of immune cells, and promoting the resolution of inflammation.

Antioxidants are compounds that can scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health. This plant contains antioxidants that can show antioxidant properties by neutralizing free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the health and function of cells.

Cancer cell proliferation involves the uncontrolled growth and division of cancer cells, leading to the development of tumors. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can inhibit cancer cell proliferation by suppressing cell growth, inducing apoptosis, and preventing the formation of new blood vessels that supply tumors with nutrients.

Pro-inflammatory cytokines are signaling molecules that promote inflammation and immune responses. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can reduce pro-inflammatory cytokines by suppressing their release, inhibiting their activity, and promoting the resolution of inflammation.

Cardioprotective effects involve the protection of the heart and cardiovascular system from damage and disease. This plant contains bioactive compounds that can exhibit cardioprotective effects by reducing blood pressure, improving blood lipid profiles, and preventing the development of cardiovascular disease.

benefits_description: Ruscus aculeatus inhibits cell growth by suppressing the expression of genes involved in cell proliferation, thereby preventing the uncontrolled division of cancer cells. The plant reduces oxidative stress by scavenging free radicals and modulating the activity of antioxidant enzymes, thus mitigating the damage caused by reactive oxygen species. Ruscus aculeatus induces apoptosis process in cancer cells by regulating the expression of pro-apoptotic proteins and activating the caspase cascade. Additionally, it inhibits tumor formation by preventing angiogenesis and suppressing the expression of genes involved in tumor progression. Ruscus aculeatus also displays antimicrobial activity against a range of pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, by interfering with their cell membrane function and DNA replication, and demonstrates anti-inflammatory effects by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Furthermore, it shows antioxidant properties by neutralizing free radicals and metal ions, exhibits cardioprotective effects by improving cardiovascular function and reducing blood pressure, and inhibits cancer cell proliferation by suppressing the expression of genes involved in cell cycle progression, and reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines by modulating the activity of immune cells.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus improve blood flow by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator that relaxes blood vessels and allows blood to flow more efficiently. This plant enhance circulation by increasing the diameter of blood vessels and improving the elasticity of the vascular walls, which helps to reduce the resistance against which the heart must pump blood. The antimicrobial and antioxidant properties of Ruscus aculeatus help to reduce blood pressure by preventing the formation of free radicals that can damage blood vessels and increase blood pressure. Ruscus aculeatus increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues by improving the flexibility of red blood cells and preventing them from becoming sticky, which allows them to pass through the microcirculation more easily. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of this plant lower cholesterol levels, increase red blood cells, prevent blood clots, reduce inflammation response, stabilize blood sugar, and improve heart function by preventing the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol and promoting the clearance of excess cholesterol from the bloodstream.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus prevents diarrhea through its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria, while also reducing inflammation by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, thus alleviating symptoms of diarrhea. The plant's antioxidant properties stimulate digestion by enhancing the activity of digestive enzymes, ultimately leading to the relaxation of intestines and alleviation of gastrointestinal discomfort. Ruscus aculeatus soothes gastrointestinal issues by protecting the mucous membrane from irritation and inflammation, promoting regular bowel movements and preventing constipation. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties cure irritable bowel syndrome by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria in the gut. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus solves gut problems and treats stomach issues by stimulating digestion, reducing inflammation, and protecting the mucous membrane, ultimately promoting a healthy digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus regulates thyroid function by influencing the secretion of thyroid hormones, which are crucial for metabolism and growth. It stabilizes adrenal function by exerting a modulating effect on the production of cortisol and other adrenal hormones, thereby reducing the risk of adrenal fatigue. Ruscus aculeatus balances insulin levels by improving insulin sensitivity, which is essential for glucose metabolism and energy production. Additionally, it lowers cortisol levels, reduces inflammation, and boosts metabolism, all of which contribute to a healthy endocrine system. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus supports adrenal function, regenerates pancreatic tissue, and normalizes hormone levels, ultimately promoting a state of homeostasis and overall well-being.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Ruscus aculeatus reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate swelling and pain associated with various integumentary disorders. This reduction in inflammation also soothes irritation, providing relief from redness and discomfort. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of Ruscus aculeatus calm eczema by neutralizing free radicals that exacerbate the condition, promoting healthy skin cell regeneration. The antimicrobial properties of this plant treat acne by killing the bacteria that cause breakouts and prevent infections. Ruscus aculeatus also relieves itching, heals wounds, prevents scars, protects skin, and fights infections due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which work in conjunction to promote tissue repair and prevent further damage.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus reduces swelling by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators, which contribute to inflammation and fluid accumulation. It improves circulation through its vasodilatory effects, increasing blood flow to the affected areas and promoting the removal of waste products. Ruscus aculeatus enhances filtration by stimulating the contraction of lymphatic vessels, which increases the rate at which fluid and waste are removed from the body. The antimicrobial properties of Ruscus aculeatus support detoxification by eliminating pathogens and toxins from the lymphatic system, boosting immunity and reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties tone the lymphatic system, increase mobility, stimulate draining, and rejuvenate the system, ultimately promoting a state of health and well-being.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Ruscus aculeatus relieves joint inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reduce oxidative stress. Ruscus aculeatus reduces muscle spasms through its muscle-relaxing effects, attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins that modulate muscle contractions and relax the nervous system. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus prevents muscle strains by improving muscle elasticity and reducing muscle fatigue, thanks to its antioxidant properties that protect muscle cells from oxidative damage and its ability to enhance muscle regeneration. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus decreases bone resorption by inhibiting osteoclast activity, an effect attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, ultimately improving bone density. Ruscus aculeatus enhances ligament strength by stimulating collagen synthesis and reducing inflammation, and also reduces arthritic swelling, increases cartilage regeneration, and soothes muscle fatigue through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus relaxes brainwaves, inducing a state of calmness, due to its flavonoid and alkaloid compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system. Ruscus aculeatus reduces anxiety by exerting anxiolytic properties, attributed to its essential oils that contain linalool and borneol, which have a calming effect on the mind. The plant enhances focus by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, thanks to its antioxidant properties that protect the brain from oxidative stress. Ruscus aculeatus soothes nerves by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the nervous system and promoting a sense of calm. By regulating the body's circadian rhythms, Ruscus aculeatus improves sleep, boosts energy, calms stress, supports mood, and regulates hormones, ultimately leading to a state of balance and well-being in the nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus regulates menstrual cycles by exerting its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms associated with irregular periods. The plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties improve fertility by protecting the reproductive system from oxidative stress and pathogens, thus creating a conducive environment for successful fertilization. Ruscus aculeatus enhances ovulation through its estrogenic activity, which stimulates the release of mature eggs from the ovaries, thereby increasing the chances of conception. By supporting hormonal balance, the plant's anti-androgenic properties reduce menstrual cramps and other symptoms related to hormonal imbalances. Moreover, Ruscus aculeatus prevents infertility by preventing the formation of fibroids and cysts, boosts testosterone levels by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase enzyme, stimulates libido through its vasodilatory effects, treats endometriosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected areas, prevents miscarriage by improving placental function, and promotes a healthy reproductive system by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus relieves congestion by its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens causing respiratory issues. This plant reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory compounds, alleviating symptoms associated with respiratory diseases. Ruscus aculeatus prevents allergies by its antihistaminic properties, reducing the body's response to allergens. Its antioxidant properties improve breathing by protecting the lungs from oxidative stress, while lessening coughing due to its expectorant properties. Ruscus aculeatus eases wheezing and calms bronchitis through its bronchodilatory properties, protecting lungs from damage and fighting infections with its immunomodulatory effects, which also alleviate asthma.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Ruscus aculeatus reduces urinary tract infections by its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria. It protects kidney function with its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, preventing oxidative stress and inflammation that can damage kidney cells. Ruscus aculeatus supports bladder health by its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and irritation in the bladder. The plant relieves urinary frequency by its diuretic properties, promoting the removal of excess fluids and waste from the body, preventing the formation of kidney stones and relieving urinary incontinence. Ruscus aculeatus improves urine flow with its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, curing urinary infections, reduces bladder irritation with its antiseptic properties, and stimulates the urinary system with its antioxidant properties.

properties_list.description: Ruscus aculeatus has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain strains of bacteria, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds in the plant.
Ruscus aculeatus has been identified as a source of antioxidants, which can help protect cells from oxidative damage and may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits.

Studies have shown that Ruscus aculeatus has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions characterized by inflammation and could be beneficial in the management of certain diseases.

Ruscus aculeatus has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, which may help relax muscles and alleviate spasms, potentially providing relief for individuals experiencing certain gastrointestinal or musculoskeletal conditions.

Ruscus aculeatus has been reported to exhibit hypotensive properties, which may help lower blood pressure, potentially contributing to the plant's use in traditional medicine for cardiovascular health.

The flavonoids present in Ruscus aculeatus may contribute to the plant's cardioprotective effects, which could help protect the heart from damage and potentially alleviate certain cardiovascular conditions.

Ruscus aculeatus has been found to exhibit uricosuric properties, which may help increase the excretion of uric acid in the urine, potentially providing relief for individuals experiencing gout or other conditions characterized by high uric acid levels.

parts_description: The most used parts of Ruscus aculeatus for medicinal purposes are its leaves. The primary bioactive compounds found in the leaves of Ruscus aculeatus are flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, as well as saponins. These compounds contribute to the leaves' anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial for treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. The flavonoids in the leaves also exhibit vasodilatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate cardiovascular issues and muscle spasms. The leaves' bioactive compounds may also possess hepatoprotective properties, helping to safeguard liver health and function.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as phenolic acids, flavonoids, and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, providing primary health benefits including cardiovascular protection, immune system modulation, and liver function improvement.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat varicose veins, hemorrhoids and heavy menstrual bleeding due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ruscus aculeatus tincture is a herbal preparation widely used to treat various conditions, including bruises, varicose veins, hemorrhoids, eczema, swollen ankles, psoriasis, edema, scars and insect bites. It is one of the most common herbal tinctures used in traditional medicine. The tincture is known to cause vasoprotective effects, helping to improve blood vessel tone and reduce inflammation. Ruscus aculeatus tincture is made by infusing roots and leaves of the Ruscus aculeatus plant in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerol. Due to the complex process of extraction and the need for precise control over the solvent's concentration, the tincture is considered challenging to manufacture.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Ruscus aculeatus roots and leaves, clean them thoroughly, and dry them in a warm, dark place.
Combine the dried roots and leaves in a 1:1 ratio and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Transfer the powdered roots and leaves to a glass jar and add a neutral solvent such as 95% ethanol or vodka to cover the mixture.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid as the Ruscus aculeatus herbal tincture.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat eczema, dermatitis, varicose veins, and bruising due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ruscus aculeatus salve is a topical preparation used to treat various skin conditions, including burns, bruises, scars, wounds, swelling, skin irritations, insect bites, stretch marks, varicose veins and eczema. It's a common treatment, widely used due to its effectiveness. One of the common effects of Ruscus aculeatus salve is its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. The salve is typically made from the roots and leaves of the Ruscus aculeatus plant, which are combined in a specific ratio to create a paste or ointment. Making Ruscus aculeatus salve can be a challenging process, as it requires precise measurements and preparation of the plant materials to ensure the desired potency and shelf life.
preparation_recipe: Harvest the roots and leaves of the Ruscus aculeatus plant and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the harvested roots and leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area to preserve their potency.
Combine the dried roots and leaves in a large pot and cover them with a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to create a herbal infusion.
Simmer the herbal infusion over low heat for several hours, allowing the plant's properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the herbal infusion and discard the solids, then allow the oil to cool and thicken before transferring it to a salve container.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and as a diuretic, and is also applied topically for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ruscus aculeatus decoction is a herbal remedy commonly used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, varicose veins, hemorrhoids, cough, fever, arthritis, and insect bites, as well as skin ulcers. It is a widely used preparation in traditional medicine due to its effectiveness in alleviating these conditions. One of its common effects is reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The preparation is typically made from the roots and leaves of the Ruscus aculeatus plant, which are combined to create the decoction. Making this preparation is moderately difficult, requiring a basic understanding of herbal medicine and the proper techniques for extracting the active ingredients from the plant material.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean Ruscus aculeatus roots and leaves.
Dry the roots and leaves thoroughly to prepare them for decoction.
Combine the dried roots and leaves in a pot to create the herbal mixture.
Add water to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for an extended period.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids, then let it cool before use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Poultice made from this plant is used to treat eczema, rheumatism, and sciatica by reducing inflammation and relieving pain.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ruscus aculeatus poultice is a preparation used to treat various conditions, including bruises, abscesses, boils, wounds, rheumatism, insect bites, sprains, and muscle strain. It's a common treatment in folk medicine, often used in conjunction with other herbal remedies. This preparation typically has a localized anti-inflammatory effect, which helps to reduce swelling and ease pain. The poultice is made by steeping the leaves and roots of Ruscus aculeatus in water, and then applying the resulting mixture to the affected area. The process of making the poultice is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbal preparation and attention to detail.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Ruscus aculeatus leaves and roots from a trusted source.
Clean the gathered leaves and roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Mash the leaves and roots together with a mortar and pestle to release their herbal properties.
Combine the mashed mixture with a small amount of water to create a smooth paste.
Apply the Ruscus aculeatus poultice directly to the affected area, securing it with a cloth if necessary.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Ruscus aculeatus are exacerbate allergies by releasing potent allergens, aggravate asthma symptoms due to airborne spores, induce skin irritation from sap or thorns, trigger severe reactions in individuals with latex hypersensitivity. Additionally, improperly using Ruscus aculeatus can worsen respiratory problems in people with pre-existing conditions, amplify the risk of plant-based anaphylaxis, provoke an immune response leading to inflammation, activate enzymes causing histamine release, stimulate the growth of new allergenic compounds, and hypersensitize individuals to plant toxins.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Pregnant women should consult a medical professional due to Ruscus aculeatus's potential toxicity.
No established safe dosage is available for Ruscus aculeatus, and consumption should be avoided.
Contact dermatitis may occur due to sap of Ruscus aculeatus if skin comes into contact with it.
Ruscus aculeatus may interact with warfarin, increasing the risk of bleeding.

High doses of Ruscus aculeatus may cause increased blood pressure to decrease, but consuming it may cause blood pressure to drop significantly in people with hypotension, and may cause orthostatic hypotension.

precautions.precaution_name: Be cautious with pregnant or breastfeeding women
precautions.precaution_list_description: Ruscus aculeatus essential oil may stimulate uterine contractions, potentially causing miscarriage or premature birth.
Prolonged use can cause liver damage due to the presence of toxic compounds like saponins and sapogenins.
Ruscus aculeatus leaves may drop due to overuse, especially if soil moisture is low.

precautions.precaution_name: Keep out of reach of children
precautions.precaution_list_description: Ruscus aculeatus sap contains toxic saponins, posing a risk of gastrointestinal upset if ingested.
Ruscus aculeatus sap is irritating to skin, mucous membranes, and eyes; proper preparation minimizes exposure.

properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Ruscus aculeatus are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which possess antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms that cause infections. The antioxidant properties of Ruscus aculeatus are associated with the presence of flavonoids, which help to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, thereby reducing the risk of chronic diseases. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ruscus aculeatus are mediated by saponins, which suppress the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating inflammation and pain. Ruscus aculeatus also exhibits antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, that help to relax smooth muscle and prevent spasms, thereby reducing the symptoms of conditions such as asthma and epilepsy. Additionally, the bioactive compounds of Ruscus aculeatus possess hypotensive, cardioprotective, uricosuric, and other medicinal properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine, with potential applications in the treatment of various cardiovascular and metabolic disorders.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Ruscus aculeatus are primarily composed of bioactive compounds such as saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, while flavonoids have been found to possess significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, coumarins present in Ruscus aculeatus have been reported to display anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory properties. Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been identified as potential antimicrobial agents, contributing to the plant's wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects. Glycosides, specifically ruscogenin and ruscic acid glycosides, have been isolated and demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities, supporting the plant's therapeutic potential. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Ruscus aculeatus contributes to its traditional use in folk medicine for the treatment of various health conditions.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Ruscus aculeatus for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions including arthritis and gout due to its anti-inflammatory properties. A salve prepared from Ruscus aculeatus is used topically to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Decoctions made from the plant's roots and rhizomes are traditionally used to treat fever and rheumatism. A poultice is also prepared from the plant's roots and applied directly to the affected area to reduce swelling and pain.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Ruscus aculeatus medicinally are consulting a medical professional to confirm its suitability for your condition, determining safe dosage to avoid toxicity, as the plant contains saponins and glycosides. Before administration, avoid allergic reactions by conducting a patch test, and check for interactions with medications, particularly blood thinners and diuretics, as Ruscus aculeatus may exacerbate their effects. Monitoring blood pressure is crucial, as the plant may lower it due to its vasodilatory properties. Be cautious with pregnant or breastfeeding women, as Ruscus aculeatus may stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development, and avoid long-term use to prevent overuse.
